betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Astrobody Data Processor§f is a compact 2×3 multiblock (TileAstrobodyDataProcessor.java:38) that consumes §6Planet ID Chips§f (advancedrocketry:planetidchip, dropped by the Observatory) and researches them in three parallel tracks — §6atmosphere§f, §6mass§f, and §6distance§f — each tracked by a separate progress bar in the GUI. Once a chip is fully researched, the planet's properties are stored in the world data and become the target destination for any rocket with a compatible ID chip.

§6Production Path (libVulpes + TileAstrobodyDataProcessor.java)§f:
- Structure: 2×3 footprint of §6Structure Blocks§f, a §6slab§f top row with a §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3) marker, a §6Power Plug§f ('P') + §6Input Hatch§f ('I') + §6Output Hatch§f ('O') on the second row, and a §6Block of Iron§f (any) on the third row that acts as the §6Data Bus§f ('D') connector
- Built via §6Crafting Table§f: 1x §6Tracking Circuit§f (advancedrocketry:ic:1), 1x §6Tin Plate§f (libvulpes:productplate:5), 1x §6Machine Structure§f (libvulpes:structuremachine), 1x §6Planet ID Chip§f (advancedrocketry:planetidchip)
- Power: 100 RF/t (set in the constructor at TileAstrobodyDataProcessor.java:60)
- Research time: 20 ticks per data track (mass, distance, atmosphere)
- Wire the §6Data Bus§f output to a §6Planet Holo Selector§f (10028) or §6Satellite§f to expose the researched data

The processor is the bridge between the §6Observatory§f (10019) and the rocket flight chain. Without it, planet ID chips are inert and you cannot target any world other than the §6Overworld§f and §6The Beyond§f (default AR dimensions). Researched chips must be inserted into a §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (10022) for the flight computer to recognize the destination.

§eTip§f: Each of the three research tracks completes in 20 ticks — but the GUI shows them as separate progress bars so you can queue three chips at once. Connect the §6Block of Iron§f in the 'D' slot to a §6Tracking Circuit§f to feed tracking data into the §6Guidance Computer§f (10028).
